title: armeria maritima var. littoralis
intro: Armeria maritima var. littoralis, also known as Sea Thrift or Beach Thrift, is a low-growing, perennial plant species that belongs to the Primrose family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Sea Thrift are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
